titleEn: Norwegian robotics firm AutoAgri rebrands as Uncrew Robotics
titleZh: 挪威机器人公司AutoAgri更名为Uncrew Robotics，战略转向多行业无人平台
summaryZh: 挪威自主车辆技术公司AutoAgri正式更名为Uncrew Robotics，标志着其从专注农业机器人向国防等多行业无人系统解决方案提供商的战略转型。公司同时推出紧凑型全电动多用途载具IC12，并强调模块化设计、全链条自研及国际市场拓展。尽管业务范围扩大，公司仍保留挪威研发生产基地。该新闻对智慧畜牧行业有一定参考价值，但核心转向通用无人平台，农业仅为其服务领域之一。
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了农业机器人企业向多行业无人平台转型的典型路径，其模块化设计、全链条自研及国际市场策略对智慧畜牧领域的设备供应商和牧场管理者具有战略参考价值。
contentFull: Norwegian robotics firm AutoAgri rebrands as Uncrew Robotics 15-07 | Autonomous vehicles | NewsLinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrintThe IC12 is an unmanned and fully electric multipurpose carrier designed for precise and cost-effective agricultural operations. Photo: Uncrew RoboticsNorwegian technology company AutoAgri, specialising in autonomous vehicles, has changed its name as it focuses increasingly on the international market and expands beyond agriculture. The company has rebranded as Uncrew Robotics, signalling a strategic shift in its direction after more than half a decade of development.Founded with a focus on autonomous agricultural machinery, the company says the new name better reflects its evolving product portfolio. The word Uncrew refers to the company’s core capability: enabling vehicles to operate without a human driver on board.According to the company, this technology delivers increased safety and operational efficiency while simultaneously reducing costs and emissions. “The new name describes the main product regardless of market segment”, the company says. The focus is increasingly on the international market, but Uncrew Robotics says it has no plans to leave Norway. All development, testing and production will continue to take place at its facility in Vanvikan, where the company says it benefits from close oversight of its entire production chain.AutoAgri IC12A recent new robot of AutoAgri is the IC12, an unmanned and fully electric multipurpose carrier designed for precise and cost-effective agricultural operations. The new model expands AutoAgri’s autonomous platform portfolio and is positioned as a more compact and flexible sibling to the company’s IC20.The IC12 is built as a multi-payload carrier supporting standard implement and payload interfaces. Its mid-mount design combines high manoeuvrability with a strong payload-to-weight ratio, while also allowing top-mounted, front-mounted, rear-mounted and trailed implements. According to AutoAgri, this flexibility makes the platform suitable for precise farming, research and development, and other specialised operations.System solutions for multiple industriesWhile Uncrew Robotics continues to serve the agricultural sector, it has broadened its offering to include system solutions for multiple industries – most notably the defence market. The company now also offers conversion services for existing traditional vehicles, retrofitting them to operate as fully or partially unmanned platforms.Modular design philosophyCentral to the company’s technology strategy is a modular design philosophy adopted since vehicle development began in 2019. Rather than assembling products from off-the-shelf components, the company has invested in developing bespoke mechanical, software and hardware electronics solutions.“This requires significant investment, but is now beginning to pay off”, CEO Jostein Sandvik says. “We continually see the benefits of maintaining close control of the value chain. Understanding the customer’s needs, and succeeding in meeting those needs through sound technological solutions that can be produced cost-effectively with the right quality – that is what it is all about.”That investment has also enabled a conversion service, allowing customers to retrofit conventional vehicles into unmanned or semi-unmanned systems using Uncrew's proprietary technology.LinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrint René Groeneveld Correspondent for AustraliaMore aboutautonomous technologyelectric tractorsMultipurpose field robotsretrofit kitsrobotic harvestingadLEARN MORERelated articles14-07 | NewsSabanto secures oversubscribed funding round, betting on smaller platforms13-07 | NewsEuropean study tour inspires Australian growers10-07 | NewsXAG launches RM80 unmanned mower, unveils new X Series drone system02-07 | NewsSabanto and Verdant Robotics integrate autonomy and precision application systems Buyers guides & Catalogues Subscribe to our YouTube channel Taiwan Smart Agriweek 2026Sep 8-10, 2026 TaiNEX 1, Tapei
translationZh:
挪威技术公司AutoAgri（专注于自主车辆）已更名，因其日益聚焦国际市场并拓展至农业以外的领域。该公司现已更名为Uncrew Robotics，标志着经过五年多的发展后，其战略方向发生转变。

该公司最初以自主农业机械为创立核心，表示新名称更能反映其不断演进的产品组合。“Uncrew”一词指代公司的核心能力：使车辆能够在没有人类驾驶员的情况下运行。

据该公司称，这项技术能够在降低成本与排放的同时，提升安全性和运营效率。“新名称描述了无论市场细分领域如何的核心产品，”公司表示。公司日益聚焦于国际市场，但Uncrew Robotics称其没有离开挪威的计划。所有开发、测试和生产将继续在其位于Vanvikan的设施中进行，公司表示，这使其能够对整个生产链保持紧密监督。

**AutoAgri IC12**

AutoAgri近期推出的新机器人是IC12，这是一款无人驾驶、全电动的多用途载具，专为精准且经济高效的农业作业而设计。新机型扩展了AutoAgri的自主平台产品组合，被定位为公司IC20的更紧凑、更灵活的姊妹产品。

IC12被设计为多载荷载具，支持标准机具接口和载荷接口。其中置设计兼具高机动性和强劲的载荷重量比，同时允许安装顶部、前部、后部及牵引式机具。据AutoAgri称，这种灵活性使该平台适用于精准农业、研发及其他专业化作业。

**面向多个行业的系统解决方案**

虽然Uncrew Robotics继续服务于农业领域，但其已拓宽产品范围，为多个行业提供系统解决方案——最值得注意的是国防市场。该公司现在还提供现有传统车辆的改装服务，将其改造为完全或部分无人平台。

**模块化设计理念**

公司技术战略的核心是自2019年车辆开发之初便采用的模块化设计理念。该公司并非从现成组件组装产品，而是投资开发定制的机械、软件和硬件电子解决方案。

“这需要大量投资，但现在开始见到回报，”首席执行官Jostein Sandvik表示。“我们不断看到保持对价值链紧密控制的好处。”
author: René Groeneveld
contentHtml: Norwegian robotics firm AutoAgri rebrands as Uncrew Robotics Norwegian technology company AutoAgri,
